steps.title: Complete the square
steps.content:
Factor out 7 from the first two terms of $y = 7x^2 - bx - 6$:
$$y = 7\left(x^2 - \frac{b}{7}x\right) - 6$$
To complete the square inside the parentheses, take half the coefficient of $x$ and square it: $\left(-\frac{b}{14}\right)^2 = \frac{b^2}{196}$. Add and subtract this value:
$$y = 7\left[\left(x - \frac{b}{14}\right)^2 - \frac{b^2}{196}\right] - 6$$
Distribute 7 and simplify $\frac{7b^2}{196} = \frac{b^2}{28}$:
$$y = 7\left(x - \frac{b}{14}\right)^2 - \frac{b^2}{28} - 6$$

steps.title: Match to the answer choice
steps.content:
Reorder the constant terms to match the answer format:
$$y = 7\left(x - \frac{b}{14}\right)^2 - 6 - \frac{b^2}{28}$$
That matches choice A — A is correct.
